data.narrative:
Code Metal, a Series B defense and security company founded by Peter Morales and Alex Showalter-Bucher, is valued at $1.25B post-money following $161M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comp universe of 10 peers—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—all operating in Defense & Security at Series B stage or equivalent maturity.

Two triangulation methods support the valuation range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-later-stage transitions to the $1.2B last post-money valuation, implying $2.4B. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same-stage companies yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a distribution ranging from $300M (low) to $3.0B (high). Named peers include Dream Security at $3.0B, Harmattan AI at $1.4B, and four companies at $1.0B each (Castelion, Defense Unicorns, Heven AeroTech).

These methods triangulate to a range of $850M (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median of $1.7B. Code Metal's current $1.25B valuation sits in the lower quartile of this range, reflecting the tension between stage-progression and peer median anchors. Key uncertainties include revenue scale relative to peers and the timing of Series C market conditions, both of which would materially affect positioning within the derived range.
